---
title: "male_models_graphs"
format: docx
date: today
editor: visual
execute:
  enabled: true
df-print: kable
project: 
  execute-dir: project
---

## Male-only Models Graphs

Load Data

```{r, include=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(knitr)
library(broom)
library(gt)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

```

```{r}

roi_fa_male <-  read_csv(here("data", "raw", "rawdata_manuscript_FA.csv")) |>
filter(gender == 0)|>
  rename(
    sus  = `Total Drug Use`
  ) |>
  filter(
    !is.na(pclr),
    !is.na(FA_mean)
  )
```

## Model 1
Partial Effects Plot
```{r}
plots_m1 <- roi_fa_male %>%
  group_by(roi) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    # Fit Model 1
    fit = map(data, ~ lm(FA_mean ~ pclr + age, data = .x)),

    # Prediction grid: pclr 0–40, age held at ROI mean
    pred = map2(data, fit, ~ {
      age_mean <- mean(.x$age, na.rm = TRUE)

      newdat <- tibble(
        pclr = seq(0, 40, by = 1),
        age  = age_mean
      )

      p <- predict(.y, newdata = newdat, se.fit = TRUE)

      newdat %>%
        mutate(
          fit = as.numeric(p$fit),
          se  = as.numeric(p$se.fit),
          lo  = fit - 1.96 * se,
          hi  = fit + 1.96 * se
        )
    }),

    # SAME STRUCTURE AS MODEL 2
    plot = pmap(list(data, pred, roi), ~
      ggplot() +
        geom_point(data = ..1, aes(x = pclr, y = FA_mean), alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_ribbon(data = ..2, aes(x = pclr, ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2) +
        geom_line(data = ..2, aes(x = pclr, y = fit), linewidth = 1) +
        coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 40), ylim = c(0.35, 0.6)) +
        labs(
          title = paste0("Model 1 (age-controlled): ", ..3),
          subtitle = "Predicted FA across PCL-R, age held at ROI mean",
          x = "PCL-R Total Score",
          y = "Mean Fractional Anisotropy (FA)"
        ) +
        theme_minimal()
    )
  ) %>%
  pull(plot)

walk(plots_m1, print)
```
## Model 2
Partial Effects Plot
```{r}

plots_m2 <- roi_fa_male %>%
  group_by(roi) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    # Fit Model 2 within each ROI
    fit = map(data, ~ lm(FA_mean ~ pclr + age + iq + sus, data = .x)),

    # Prediction grid: pclr from 0 to 40, other covariates held at ROI means
    pred = map2(data, fit, ~ {
      age_mean <- mean(.x$age, na.rm = TRUE)
      iq_mean  <- mean(.x$iq,  na.rm = TRUE)
      sus_mean <- mean(.x$sus, na.rm = TRUE)

      newdat <- tibble(
        pclr = seq(0, 40, by = 1),
        age  = age_mean,
        iq   = iq_mean,
        sus  = sus_mean
      )

      p <- predict(.y, newdata = newdat, se.fit = TRUE)

      newdat %>%
        mutate(
          fit = as.numeric(p$fit),
          se  = as.numeric(p$se.fit),
          lo  = fit - 1.96 * se,
          hi  = fit + 1.96 * se
        )
    }),

    # Plot: raw points + age/iq/sus-controlled prediction line + 95% CI ribbon
    # IMPORTANT FIX: roi is NOT inside nested data after nest(), so pass it in via pmap
    plot = pmap(list(data, pred, roi), ~
      ggplot() +
        geom_point(data = ..1, aes(x = pclr, y = FA_mean), alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_ribbon(data = ..2, aes(x = pclr, ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2) +
        geom_line(data = ..2, aes(x = pclr, y = fit), linewidth = 1) +
        coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 40), ylim = c(0.35, 0.6)) +
        labs(
          title = paste0("Model 2 (covariate-controlled): ", ..3),
          subtitle = "Predicted FA across PCL-R, age, IQ, and SUS held at ROI means",
          x = "PCL-R Total Score",
          y = "Mean Fractional Anisotropy (FA)"
        ) +
        theme_minimal()
    )
  ) %>%
  pull(plot)

walk(plots_m2, print)
```
